summary: Panel-gate enforcement is genuinely intact after retiring the PreToolUse hook backstop. (1) The in-binary gate is the SOLE and AUTHORITATIVE enforcer and is byte-unchanged: git diff main...HEAD touches NO code in internal/complete, internal/panel, or internal/config — panelGate (panel_advisory.go) still runs at complete.go step 2.25 BEFORE CommitAll/bd-close/merge, returning guard.NewFailure on Block having mutated nothing. TestPanelGate (internal/complete, 3.33s) and TestPanelGateDecision (internal/panel, 0.20s) both PASS on fresh -count=1 runs. internal/complete does NOT import internal/hook (verified via go list Imports), so the gate is fully independent of any hook install. (2) Migration footgun is benign: the retired 'pre-complete' name is gone from hook.Names and dispatch.go, so `mindspec hook pre-complete` now returns a plain fmt.Errorf 'unknown hook'; otelExitCode (otel.go) maps it to 1 (not codedError/usageError/workloadExitError). Live trace confirmed EXIT=1 — and only exit 2 blocks a Claude Code PreToolUse tool call, so a leftover entry never blocks Bash. removeStaleMindspecEntries strips the leftover on next setup (isMindspecOwned keys on the 'mindspec hook ' prefix; wanted PreToolUse set is now empty), with test witnesses TestWantedHooks_NoPreCompletePreToolUse and TestRunClaude_CleansStalePreToolUse. (3) No enforcement gap: the gate is binary-resident, always runs on `mindspec complete`, independent of hook install. The MINDSPEC_SKIP_PANEL env hatch and enforcement.panel_gate config toggle are unchanged and intentional. (4) Orchestrator enforcement is unaffected — it relies on the in-binary gate; the hook was a Claude-routed-only backstop. NON-BLOCKING NIT (not a safety issue): several doc comments in panel_advisory.go/complete.go are now stale ('Hard enforcement stays at the hook layer alone (HC-4)', 'the hook is now a defense-in-depth backstop', advisory label mentions 'the hook also checks staleness + dirty tree'). Comments only; no behavioral impact.
